title: Tomato and Onion Salad – honest food
excerpt: Making a tomato and onion salad doesn't take gourmet knowledge. The knowledge lies in the dressing and in making it early enough for the dressing to soak into the onions. On its own she's lovely. Alongside curry or braaivleis she's better. I make it in the afternoon …

seo.title: Tomato and Onion Salad - honest food - Marinda Kook

html:
Making a tomato and onion salad doesn't take gourmet knowledge. The knowledge lies in the dressing and in making it early enough for the dressing to soak into the onions. On its own she's lovely. Alongside curry or braaivleis she's better. I make it in the afternoon for the evening's meal. To me it's an honest, unpretentious salad without any fuss.

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 6 tomatoes
  • 1 onion
  • 160 ml grape vinegar
  • 80 ml oil (not olive oil)
  • 15 ml sugar
  • salt and white pepper

NOW:

  1. wash the tomatoes and slice them into thin rings – put into the salad bowl
  2. slice the onions into rings and put on top of the tomato – mix together. Now salt and pepper the tomato and onion to taste.
  3. mix the oil, vinegar and sugar and whisk until it forms an emulsion
  4. pour over the salad and cover with cling wrap – put in the fridge

You know the salad was good when someone drinks up the dressing and sits back looking pleased with themselves.
